title: Calculator App
desc: Build a fully functional command-line calculator with all basic operations, history tracking, and error handling.
what_you_build: A command-line calculator that supports +, -, *, /, %, ** operations with input validation, calculation history, and a clean menu interface.
what_you_learn: How to structure a Python project
folder_structure:
calculator/
├── main.py          # Entry point
├── calculator.py    # Core logic
├── history.py       # History management
├── requirements.txt # (empty for this project)
└── README.md        # Documentation

steps.title: Setup Project Structure
steps.desc: Create the folder and files. Initialize git repository.
steps.code:
# Terminal commands
mkdir calculator
cd calculator
git init
touch main.py calculator.py README.md

# .gitignore
echo '__pycache__/' > .gitignore
echo '*.pyc' >> .gitignore
steps.hint: Always initialize git at the START of a project, not the end!

steps.title: Build Core Calculator Functions
steps.desc: Write functions for each operation. Keep them simple and focused.
steps.hint: Each function does ONE thing. This makes testing and debugging much easier.

steps.title: Add Input Validation
steps.desc: Handle bad input gracefully — users will always type something wrong!
steps.hint: Use while True + try/except for input validation. This is the standard Python pattern.

steps.title: Build the Main Loop
steps.desc: Create the main program loop with menu and history.
steps.hint: The if __name__ == '__main__': guard means this only runs when you execute main.py directly, not when imported.

steps.title: Write README and Push to GitHub
steps.desc: Document your project and push to GitHub for your portfolio.
steps.code:
# README.md content:
# # Python Calculator
# A command-line calculator built with Python.
# 
# ## Features
# - Basic operations: +, -, *, /, **, %
# - Input validation
# - Calculation history
# - Clean menu interface
# 
# ## How to Run
# python main.py
# 
# ## What I Learned
# - Function design
# - Error handling
# - Input validation

# Git commands:
# git add .
# git commit -m 'feat: complete calculator with history'
# git push origin main
steps.hint: A good README is what makes recruiters notice your project. Always include: what it does, how to run it, what you learned.

extensions: Add scientific functions (sin, cos, sqrt) using math module
Save history to a file so it persists between sessions
Build a web version using Flask
github_readme_template:
# Python Calculator

A command-line calculator with history tracking.

## Features
- Basic arithmetic: +, -, *, /, **, %
- Input validation and error handling
- Calculation history

## Run
```bash
python main.py
```

## Built With
- Python 3.x
- No external dependencies
